name: notion-create-attachment
description:
Create a small UTF-8 text attachment and upload it to Notion. Use this tool for text-based artifacts that you can provide directly as a string, such as HTML, Markdown, plain text, CSV, JSON, XML, CSS, YAML, TSV, calendar, GPX, or SVG files. The filename must use a supported extension. The optional content_type is a MIME type and must agree with the filename; omit it to infer the type from the extension. This tool does not read local filesystem paths, fetch URLs, decode base64, or upload binary files. Inline content is limited to 200 KiB after UTF-8 encoding. If this tool is unavailable, or for larger or binary files, use `ntn files create` or the Notion File Upload API instead. The response includes a `markdown_source` value. To place the uploaded file on a page, pass that source to `create-pages` or `update-page`. Attach it within one hour: unattached uploads remain temporary and are deleted after they expire. Use `` for HTML files so Notion renders the sandboxed preview; use `` for other text files. 1. Create an HTML artifact: {"filename":"report.html","content":"

Report

"} 2. Create Markdown with an explicit MIME type: {"filename":"notes.md","content_type":"text/markdown","content":"# Notes Hello"}

filename.description: The filename to create in Notion, including a supported text-file extension such as .html, .md, or .txt.

content.description: The complete UTF-8 text content of the file. Maximum 200 KiB after UTF-8 encoding.

content_type.description: Optional MIME type, such as text/html or text/markdown. It must match the filename extension. Omit it to infer the type from the filename.
